The facts relevant io the present criminali bail application are as a "On 1o-o5-9o93, at s33o houls, Syed Safdar Hussain Rizui' a resident of llouse No. 75-C, Sector 35/A, Korangi No' 4' Karachi, reported that his son, lzhar llussain, and his, f ientl Ifamza vire standing outside a company House No'C-25' Sector s1/A, Compiunrl Area, Eoraigi No'4, when three unknown indiuiduais on a motorcyclq attumPted to 'enatch their mobile phones. When Azhar resisted, one of the robbers fired at him with a pistol, causing hifi to fall injured' The suspects fled the ""un. on theil motortycle' Azhar -was taken to Jinnah HosPital but succumbed lo his injuries.during treatment. Syed Safdar l{ussain Rizvi, who had been ' in Istaruabad foi business, returned and ley'rned of his son's death' He lodged FIR against the thrce unidentifi.ed to'bbers',.qu."t-irg lugul action for the murder of his son"' 5.

When Azhar resisted, one of the robbers fired at him with a pistol, causing hifi to fall injured' The suspects fled the ""un. on theil motortycle' Azhar -was taken to Jinnah HosPital but succumbed lo his injuries.during treatment. Syed Safdar l{ussain Rizvi, who had been ' in Istaruabad foi business, returned and ley'rned of his son's death' He lodged FIR against the thrce unidentifi.ed to'bbers',.qu."t-irg lugul action for the murder of his son"' 5. The learned counsel for the Applicant hds argued that there is a delay ofzr hours in lodging the FIR without 'nj "*pluttutio"' despite the police station being only 1'5 km from the iricident site in a thicldy populated area. He further contends that thelapplicant's name is not H C ! I O )t;( I v foliows: t21 $ J.,l. a. mentionedintheFlP.,andthereisnoidentificationparadeortimely submissionofevidence,suggestingpreplanninginthiscase.Heargues that the applicant's invoivement is based solelJ on 4nother FIR' where the accused was arrested in the same police station' lndicating a pattern of preplanning. He further contends that the incident occurred on 1o-o5- 2093, but the FIR was lodged on 11-o5-eo93' and witness statements under Section 16r Cr'P'C' were recorded on' 15-05-2099' with no explanation for the delays' He contends that no ciime weapon' such as a pistol, was recovered from the aPplicant' and there lare no eye witnesses as per the FIR. He further argues that the witnesses Jtt "los" relatives of the deceased, and the complainant was not present during the incident' making the case higlily doubtfirl and fit for furthel inquiry under Section a97 Cr.P.C. He contends that the applicant haslno prior convictions <ir pending cases and has been falsely implicated with malafrde intentions by thepoliceincollusionwiththecomplainant.ilnlightoftheabove arguments and. case laws, he prays that this Hlnorable Court may be pleased to grant bail to the applicant in the interest ofjustice +. The learned Deputy Prosecutor Genetat (OfG1 opposed the bail application and argued that the delay in lodgfng the FIR does not undermine the prosecutiol's case' as the complainant was in Islamabad and returned to Karachi upon learning of his lon's death He further contends that the absence of the applicant's narhe in the FIR does not absolve him, as the investigation revealed hislinvolvement $ated on evidence and witness statements' He argues that (he recovery ofthe crime I weapon is not always necessary for establishing it'ilt' and the presence of close relatives as witnesses does not discredit thdir testimony' He €urther contends that the applicant's involvement is supforted by his arrest in a related FlR, indicating a Pattern of criminal beliavior' He contends that I the delay in recording witness statements under dection 161 Cr'P'C' is not c on C)

He contends that the applicant haslno prior convictions <ir pending cases and has been falsely implicated with malafrde intentions by thepoliceincollusionwiththecomplainant.ilnlightoftheabove arguments and. case laws, he prays that this Hlnorable Court may be pleased to grant bail to the applicant in the interest ofjustice +. The learned Deputy Prosecutor Genetat (OfG1 opposed the bail application and argued that the delay in lodgfng the FIR does not undermine the prosecutiol's case' as the complainant was in Islamabad and returned to Karachi upon learning of his lon's death He further contends that the absence of the applicant's narhe in the FIR does not absolve him, as the investigation revealed hislinvolvement $ated on evidence and witness statements' He argues that (he recovery ofthe crime I weapon is not always necessary for establishing it'ilt' and the presence of close relatives as witnesses does not discredit thdir testimony' He €urther contends that the applicant's involvement is supforted by his arrest in a related FlR, indicating a Pattern of criminal beliavior' He contends that I the delay in recording witness statements under dection 161 Cr'P'C' is not c on C) I I I3I unusual in complex investigations' He further argqes that the applicant's prior criminal record, if any, and the gravity of thJ o{fense' including the murder of the deceased, warrant dismissal of bail] to ensure justice and prevent tamPering with evidence or inlluencingi witnesses Lastly' he prayed that the bail application should be dismilsed in the interest of justice.: 5. Upon careful consideration of the argumJnts advanced by both sides and a meticu]ous examination ofthe material pvailable on record, the following findings and reasons are t""oldudt Although the I Applicant/Accused is not named in the FIR' whlch was lodged against unknown persons, the record reveals that he' hong with co-accused i.. Shahid, was arrested on 1l-o5-9o23 in connectibn with another crime I (FIR No.282l2o99) under Sectio ns 35g, 324, and 3f PPC' Eyewitnesses of the present crime identified the Applicant/A""uttJ "t the lockup as one of the culprits, and he confessed to his iivolvemint in the presence of I eyewitnesses and the complainant This is furthLr corroborated by the statements of prosecution witnesses, includingl the complainant and another private witness, recorded under Section 161 Cr'P C' Additionally' the empty cartridge recovered from the crime sfene matches the pistol recovered from the Applicant/Accused at the tim! ofhis arrest" as per the l ballistic expert rePort, strongly supporting the prosecution's case' The heinous nature of the crime, involving "tt*pt"{f robbery and the fatal shooting of the complainant's son, Azhar Husszlin' places it within the prohibitory clause of Section 'l97 Cr'P C, makihg it non-bailable The ?i defense has not alleged.any mala-frde on the p[rt of the complainant' I police, or prosecution witnesses' further strengtilening the Prosecution's I case.

Upon careful consideration of the argumJnts advanced by both sides and a meticu]ous examination ofthe material pvailable on record, the following findings and reasons are t""oldudt Although the I Applicant/Accused is not named in the FIR' whlch was lodged against unknown persons, the record reveals that he' hong with co-accused i.. Shahid, was arrested on 1l-o5-9o23 in connectibn with another crime I (FIR No.282l2o99) under Sectio ns 35g, 324, and 3f PPC' Eyewitnesses of the present crime identified the Applicant/A""uttJ "t the lockup as one of the culprits, and he confessed to his iivolvemint in the presence of I eyewitnesses and the complainant This is furthLr corroborated by the statements of prosecution witnesses, includingl the complainant and another private witness, recorded under Section 161 Cr'P C' Additionally' the empty cartridge recovered from the crime sfene matches the pistol recovered from the Applicant/Accused at the tim! ofhis arrest" as per the l ballistic expert rePort, strongly supporting the prosecution's case' The heinous nature of the crime, involving "tt*pt"{f robbery and the fatal shooting of the complainant's son, Azhar Husszlin' places it within the prohibitory clause of Section 'l97 Cr'P C, makihg it non-bailable The ?i defense has not alleged.any mala-frde on the p[rt of the complainant' I police, or prosecution witnesses' further strengtilening the Prosecution's I case. The arguments raised by the learied counsel for the Applicant/Accused, based on a deeper appreciation of evidence' are not r. i (* s*-. * 6HC o On warranted at this bail stage, as the Court's role il limited to determining L I [41 t: whether a prima facie case exists ln light bf these findings' the Applicant/Accused is actively involved in the alleged offense' and the gravity of the crime, coupled with the evidence 'on record' justifies the denial of bail. This Court, in the case ot Aijaz 'fli u' rnt State (coo, YLR fi6o), has previously ruled under similar circumstances that // granting bail to individuals accused of robbery {or:ld undermine public safe$r and create a sense ofinsecurity within sociedy' 6. In light of the foregoing reasons, the presdnt bail apPlication filed on behalf of the Applicant, being devoid of subsfantive merit' is hereby dismissed. The observations made in this Order are limited to the.a adjudication of this bail application and will rlot affect the rights of ther party during the trial' These are the reasfns for the short Order da ted: l3-O3-2O95.
